Time is a test of trouble,
text:
But not a remedy.
If such it prove, it prove too
There was no malady.

title: Poem 1788
text: IV.

title: We cover thee, sweet face.
text:
Not that we tire of thee,
But that thyself fatigue of us;
Remember, as thou flee,
We follow thee until
Thou notice us no more,
And then, reluctant, turn away
To con thee o'er and o'er,
And blame the scanty love
We were content to show,
Augmented, sweet, a hundred fold
If thou would'st take it now.

title: Poem 1790
text: V.

title: Poem 1791
text: ENDING.

title: That is solemn we have ended, –
text:
Be it but a play,
Or a glee among the garrets,
Or a holiday,

title: Or a leaving home; or later,
text:
Parting with a world
We have understood, for better
Still it be unfurled.

title: Poem 1794
text: VI.

title: The stimulus, beyond the grave
text:
His countenance to see,
Supports me like imperial drams
Afforded royally.

title: Poem 1796
text: VII.

title: Given in marriage unto thee,
text:
Oh, thou celestial host!
Bride of the Father and the Son,
Bride of the Holy Ghost!

title: Other betrothal shall dissolve,
Poem 1799
text: VIII.

title: That such have died enables us
Poem 1801
text: IX.

title: They won't frown always, – some sweet day
text:
When I forget to tease,
They'll recollect how cold I looked,
And how I just said 'please.'

title: Then they will hasten to the door
text:
To call the little child,
Who cannot thank them, for the ice
That on her lisping piled.

title: Poem 1804
text: X.

title: Poem 1805
text: IMMORTALITY.

title: It is an honorable thought,
text:
And makes one lift one's hat,
As one encountered gentlefolk
Upon a daily street,

title: That we've immortal place,
text:
Though pyramids decay,
And kingdoms, like the orchard,
Flit russetly away.

title: Poem 1808
text: XI.

title: The distance that the dead have gone
And then, that we have followed them
text:
We more than half suspect,
So intimate have we become
With their dear retrospect.

title: Poem 1811
text: XII.

title: How dare the robins sing,
text:
When men and women hear
Who since they went to their account
Have settled with the year! –
Paid all that life had earned
In one consummate bill,
And now, what life or death can do
Is immaterial.
Insulting is the sun
To him whose mortal light,
Beguiled of immortality,
Bequeaths him to the night.
In deference to him
Extinct be every hum,
Whose garden wrestles with the dew,
At daybreak overcome!

title: Poem 1813
text: XIII.

title: Poem 1814
text: DEATH.

title: Death is like the insect
text:
Menacing the tree,
Competent to kill it,
But decoyed may be.

title: Bait it with the balsam,
text:
Seek it with the knife,
Baffle, if it cost you
Everything in life.

title: Then, if it have burrowed
text:
Out of reach of skill,
Ring the tree and leave it, –
'T is the vermin's will.

title: Poem 1818
text: XIV.

title: Poem 1819
text: UNWARNED.

title: 'T is sunrise, little maid, hast thou
text:
No station in the day?
'T was not thy wont to hinder so, –
Retrieve thine industry.

title: 'T is noon, my little maid, alas!
text:
And art thou sleeping yet?
The lily waiting to be wed,
The bee, dost thou forget?

title: My little maid, 't is night; alas,
text:
That night should be to thee
Instead of morning! Hadst thou broached
Thy little plan to me,
Dissuade thee if I could not, sweet,
I might have aided thee.

title: Poem 1823
text: XV.

title: Each that we lose takes part of us;
text:
A crescent still abides,
Which like the moon, some turbid night,
Is summoned by the tides.

title: Poem 1825
text: XVI.

title: Not any higher stands the grave
This latest leisure equal lulls
Poem 1828
text: XVII.

title: Poem 1829
text: ASLEEP.

title: As far from pity as complaint,
text:
As cool to speech as stone,
As numb to revelation
As if my trade were bone.

title: As far from time as history,
text:
As near yourself to–day
As children to the rainbow's scarf,
Or sunset's yellow play

title: To eyelids in the sepulchre.
text:
How still the dancer lies,
While color's revelations break,
And blaze the butterflies!

title: Poem 1833
text: XVIII.

title: Poem 1834
text: THE SPIRIT.

title: 'T is whiter than an Indian pipe,
Not any voice denotes it here,
This limitless hyperbole
Poem 1838
text: XIX.

title: Poem 1839
text: THE MONUMENT.

title: She laid her docile crescent down,
text:
And this mechanic stone
Still states, to dates that have forgot,
The news that she is gone.

title: So constant to its stolid trust,
text:
The shaft that never knew,
It shames the constancy that fled
Before its emblem flew.

title: Poem 1842
text: XX.

title: Bless God, he went as soldiers,
Please God, might I behold him
text:
In epauletted white,
I should not fear the foe then,
I should not fear the fight.

title: Poem 1845
text: XXI.

title: Immortal is an ample word
text:
When what we need is by,
But when it leaves us for a time,
'T is a necessity.

title: Of heaven above the firmest proof
text:
We fundamental know,
Except for its marauding hand,
It had been heaven below.

title: Poem 1848
text: XXII.

title: Where every bird is bold to go,
text:
And bees abashless play,
The foreigner before he knocks
Must thrust the tears away.

title: Poem 1850
text: XXIII.

title: The grave my little cottage is,
text:
Where, keeping house for thee,
I make my parlor orderly,
And lay the marble tea,

title: For two divided, briefly,
text:
A cycle, it may be,
Till everlasting life unite
In strong society.

title: Poem 1853
text: XXIV.

title: This was in the white of the year,
text:
That was in the green,
Drifts were as difficult then to think
As daisies now to be seen.

title: Looking back is best that is left,
text:
Or if it be before,
Retrospection is prospect's half,
Sometimes almost more.

title: Poem 1856
text: XXV.

title: Sweet hours have perished here;
Poem 1858
text: XXVI.

title: Me! Come! My dazzled face
text: In such a shining place!

title: Me! Hear! My foreign ear
text: The sounds of welcome near!

title: The saints shall meet
text: Our bashful feet.

title: My holiday shall be
text: That they remember me;

title: My paradise, the fame
text: That they pronounce my name.

title: Poem 1864
text: XXVII.

title: Poem 1865
text: INVISIBLE.

title: From us she wandered now a year,
No eye hath seen and lived,
text:
We ignorant must be.
We only know what time of year
We took the mystery.

title: Poem 1868
text: XXVIII.

title: I wish I knew that woman's name,
text:
So, when she comes this way,
To hold my life, and hold my ears,
For fear I hear her say

title: She's 'sorry I am dead,' again,
text:
Just when the grave and I
Have sobbed ourselves almost to sleep, –
Our only lullaby.

title: Poem 1871
text: XXIX.

title: Poem 1872
text: TRYING TO FORGET.

title: Bereaved of all, I went abroad,
text:
No less bereaved to be
Upon a new peninsula, –
The grave preceded me,

title: Obtained my lodgings ere myself,
text:
And when I sought my bed,
The grave it was, reposed upon
The pillow for my head.

title: I waked, to find it first awake,
In cups of artificial drowse
text:
To sleep its shape away, –
The grave was finished, but the spade
Remained in memory.

title: Poem 1877
text: XXX.

title: I felt a funeral in my brain,
text:
And mourners, to and fro,
Kept treading, treading, till it seemed
That sense was breaking through.

title: And when they all were seated,
text:
A service like a drum
Kept beating, beating, till I thought
My mind was going numb.

title: And then I heard them lift a box,
text:
And creak across my soul
With those same boots of lead, again.
Then space began to toll

title: As all the heavens were a bell,
text:
And Being but an ear,
And I and silence some strange race,
Wrecked, solitary, here.

title: Poem 1882
text: XXXI.

title: I meant to find her when I came;
I meant to tell her how I longed
To wander now is my abode;
text:
To rest, – to rest would be
A privilege of hurricane
To memory and me.

title: Poem 1886
text: XXXII.

title: Poem 1887
text: WAITING.

title: I sing to use the waiting,
Till, his best step approaching,
text:
We journey to the day,
And tell each other how we sang
To keep the dark away.

title: Poem 1890
text: XXXIII.

title: A sickness of this world it most occasions
A chief indifference, as foreign
text:
A world must be
Themselves forsake contented,
For Deity.

title: Poem 1893
text: XXXIV.

title: Superfluous were the sun
That syllable whose faith
text:
Just saves it from despair,
And whose 'I'll meet you' hesitates
If love inquire, 'Where?'

title: Upon his dateless fame
text:
Our periods may lie,
As stars that drop anonymous
From an abundant sky.

title: Poem 1897
text: XXXV.

title: So proud she was to die
text:
It made us all ashamed
That what we cherished, so unknown
To her desire seemed.

title: So satisfied to go
text:
Where none of us should be,
Immediately, that anguish stooped
Almost to jealousy.

title: Poem 1900
text: XXXVI.

title: Poem 1901
text: FAREWELL.

title: Tie the strings to my life, my Lord,
text:
Then I am ready to go!
Just a look at the horses –
Rapid! That will do!

title: Put me in on the firmest side,
But never I mind the bridges,
Good–by to the life I used to live,
Poem 1906
text: XXXVII.

title: The dying need but little, dear, –
text:
A glass of water's all,
A flower's unobtrusive face
To punctuate the wall,

title: A fan, perhaps, a friend's regret,
text:
And certainly that one
No color in the rainbow
Perceives when you are gone.

title: Poem 1909
text: XXXVIII.

title: Poem 1910
text: DEAD.

title: There's something quieter than sleep
text:
Within this inner room!
It wears a sprig upon its breast,
And will not tell its name.

title: Some touch it and some kiss it,
While simple–hearted neighbors
text:
Chat of the 'early dead,'
We, prone to periphrasis,
Remark that birds have fled!

title: Poem 1914
text: XXXIX.

title: The soul should always stand ajar,
text:
That if the heaven inquire,
He will not be obliged to wait,
Or shy of troubling her.

title: Depart, before the host has slid
text:
The bolt upon the door,
To seek for the accomplished guest, –
Her visitor no more.

title: Poem 1917
text: XL.

title: Three weeks passed since I had seen her, –
And a company – our pleasure
Borne, without dissent of either,
Poem 1921
text: XLI.

title: I breathed enough to learn the trick,
text:
And now, removed from air,
I simulate the breath so well,
That one, to be quite sure

title: The lungs are stirless, must descend
text:
Among the cunning cells,
And touch the pantomime himself.
How cool the bellows feels!

title: Poem 1924
text: XLII.

title: I wonder if the sepulchre
text:
Is not a lonesome way,
When men and boys, and larks and June
Go down the fields to hay!

title: Poem 1926
text: XLIII.

title: Poem 1927
text: JOY IN DEATH.

title: If tolling bell I ask the cause.
That bells should joyful ring to tell
text:
A soul had gone to heaven,
Would seem to me the proper way
A good news should be given.

title: Poem 1930
text: XLIV.

title: If I may have it when it's dead
Until they lock it in the grave,
text:
'T is bliss I cannot weigh,
For though they lock thee in the grave,
Myself can hold the key.

title: Think of it, lover! I and thee
Poem 1934
text: XLV.

title: Before the ice is in the pools,
text:
Before the skaters go,
Or any cheek at nightfall
Is tarnished by the snow,

title: Before the fields have finished,
text:
Before the Christmas tree,
Wonder upon wonder
Will arrive to me!

title: What we touch the hems of
That which sings so, speaks so,
text:
When there's no one here, –
Will the frock I wept in
Answer me to wear?

title: Poem 1939
text: XLVI.

title: Poem 1940
text: DYING.

title: I heard a fly buzz when I died;
text:
The stillness round my form
Was like the stillness in the air
Between the heaves of storm.

title: The eyes beside had wrung them dry,
text:
And breaths were gathering sure
For that last onset, when the king
Be witnessed in his power.

title: I willed my keepsakes, signed away
text:
What portion of me I
Could make assignable, – and then
There interposed a fly,

title: With blue, uncertain, stumbling buzz,
Poem 1945
text: XLVII.

title: Adrift! A little boat adrift!
text:
And night is coming down!
Will no one guide a little boat
Unto the nearest town?

title: So sailors say, on yesterday,
text:
Just as the dusk was brown,
One little boat gave up its strife,
And gurgled down and down.

title: But angels say, on yesterday,
text:
Just as the dawn was red,
One little boat o'erspent with gales
Retrimmed its masts, redecked its sails
Exultant, onward sped!

title: Poem 1949
text: XLVIII.

title: There's been a death in the opposite house
text:
As lately as to–day.
I know it by the numb look
Such houses have alway.

title: The neighbors rustle in and out,
Somebody flings a mattress out, –
The minister goes stiffly in
And then the milliner, and the man
text:
Of the appalling trade,
To take the measure of the house.
There'll be that dark parade

title: Of tassels and of coaches soon;
text:
It's easy as a sign, –
The intuition of the news
In just a country town.

title: Poem 1956
text: XLIX.

title: We never know we go, – when we are going
Poem 1958
text: L.

title: Poem 1959
text: THE SOUL'S STORM.

title: It struck me every day
text:
The lightning was as new
As if the cloud that instant slit
And let the fire through.

title: It burned me in the night,
I thought that storm was brief, –
Poem 1963
text: LI.

title: Water is taught by thirst;
Poem 1965
text: LII.

title: Poem 1966
text: THIRST.

title: We thirst at first, – 't is Nature's act;
text:
And later, when we die,
A little water supplicate
Of fingers going by.

title: It intimates the finer want,
text:
Whose adequate supply
Is that great water in the west
Termed immortality.

title: Poem 1969
text: LIII.

title: A clock stopped – not the mantel's;
text:
Geneva's farthest skill
Can't put the puppet bowing
That just now dangled still.

title: An awe came on the trinket!
text:
The figures hunched with pain,
Then quivered out of decimals
Into degreeless noon.

title: It will not stir for doctors,
Nods from the gilded pointers,
text:
Nods from the seconds slim,
Decades of arrogance between
The dial life and him.

title: Poem 1974
text: LIV.

title: Poem 1975
text: CHARLOTTE BRONTË'S GRAVE.

title: All overgrown by cunning moss,
text:
All interspersed with weed,
The little cage of 'Currer Bell,'
In quiet Haworth laid.

title: This bird, observing others,
text:
When frosts too sharp became,
Retire to other latitudes,
Quietly did the same,

title: But differed in returning;
text:
Since Yorkshire hills are green,
Yet not in all the nests I meet
Can nightingale be seen.

title: Gathered from many wanderings,
text:
Gethsemane can tell
Through what transporting anguish
She reached the asphodel!

title: Soft fall the sounds of Eden
Poem 1981
text: LV.

title: A toad can die of light!
text:
Death is the common right
Of toads and men, –
Of earl and midge
The privilege.
Why swagger then?
The gnat's supremacy
Is large as thine.

title: Poem 1983
text: LVI.

title: Far from love the Heavenly Father
Oftener by the claw of dragon
text:
Than the hand of friend,
Guides the little one predestined
To the native land.

title: Poem 1986
text: LVII.

title: Poem 1987
text: SLEEPING.

title: A long, long sleep, a famous sleep
text:
That makes no show for dawn
By stretch of limb or stir of lid, –
An independent one.

title: Was ever idleness like this?
text:
Within a hut of stone
To bask the centuries away
Nor once look up for noon?

title: Poem 1990
text: LVIII.

title: Poem 1991
text: RETROSPECT.

title: 'T was just this time last year I died.
text:
I know I heard the corn,
When I was carried by the farms, –
It had the tassels on.

title: I thought how yellow it would look
I thought just how red apples wedged
I wondered which would miss me least,
text:
And when Thanksgiving came,
If father'd multiply the plates
To make an even sum.

title: And if my stocking hung too high,
text:
Would it blur the Christmas glee,
That not a Santa Claus could reach
The altitude of me?

title: But this sort grieved myself, and so
text:
I thought how it would be
When just this time, some perfect year,
Themselves should come to me.

title: Poem 1998
text: LIX.

title: Poem 1999
text: ETERNITY.

title: On this wondrous sea,
text:
Sailing silently,
Ho! pilot, ho!
Knowest thou the shore
Where no breakers roar,
Where the storm is o'er?

title: In the silent west
text:
Many sails at rest,
Their anchors fast;
Thither I pilot thee, –
Land, ho! Eternity!
Ashore at last!

title: Index of First Lines
text:
A bird came down the walk:
A charm invests a face
A clock stopped – not the mantel's;
A death–blow is a life–blow to some
A deed knocks first at thought,
A dew sufficed itself
A door just opened on a street –
A drop fell on the apple tree,
A face devoid of love or grace,
A lady red upon the hill
A light exists in spring
A little road not made of man,
A long, long sleep, a famous sleep
A modest lot, a fame petite,
A murmur in the trees to note,
A narrow fellow in the grass
A poor torn heart, a tattered heart,
A precious, mouldering pleasure 't is
A route of evanescence
A sepal, petal, and a thorn
A shady friend for torrid days
A sickness of this world it most occasions
A sloop of amber slips away
A solemn thing it was, I said,
A something in a summer's day,
A spider sewed at night
A thought went up my mind to–day
A throe upon the features
A toad can die of light!
A word is dead
A wounded deer leaps highest,
Adrift! A little boat adrift!
Afraid? Of whom am I afraid?
After a hundred years
All overgrown by cunning moss,
Alter? When the hills do.
Ample make this bed.
An altered look about the hills;
An awful tempest mashed the air,
An everywhere of silver,
Angels in the early morning
Apparently with no surprise
Arcturus is his other name, –
Are friends delight or pain?
As by the dead we love to sit,
As children bid the guest good–night,
As far from pity as complaint,
As if some little Arctic flower,
As imperceptibly as grief
Ashes denote that fire was;
At half–past three a single bird
At last to be identified!
At least to pray is left, is left.
Because I could not stop for Death,
Before I got my eye put out,
Before the ice is in the pools,
Before you thought of spring,
Belshazzar had a letter, –
Bereaved of all, I went abroad,
Besides the autumn poets sing,
Blazing in gold and quenching in purple,
Bless God, he went as soldiers,
Bring me the sunset in a cup,
Come slowly, Eden!
Could I but ride indefinite,
Could mortal lip divine
Dare you see a soul at the white heat?
Dear March, come in!
Death is a dialogue between
Death is like the insect
Death sets a thing significant
Delayed till she had ceased to know,
Delight becomes pictorial
Departed to the judgment,
Did the harebell loose her girdle
Doubt me, my dim companion!
Drab habitation of whom?
Drowning is not so pitiful
Each life converges to some centre
Each that we lose takes part of us;
Elysium is as far as to
Essential oils are wrung:
Except the heaven had come so near,
Except to heaven, she is nought;
Experiment to me
Exultation is the going
Far from love the Heavenly Father
Farther in summer than the birds,
Fate slew him, but he did not drop;
Father, I bring thee not myself, –
Few get enough, – enough is one;
Finite to fail, but infinite to venture.
For each ecstatic instant
Forbidden fruit a flavor has
Frequently the woods are pink,
From all the jails the boys and girls
From cocoon forth a butterfly
From us she wandered now a year,
Given in marriage unto thee,
Glee! The great storm is over!
God gave a loaf to every bird,
God made a little gentian;
God permits industrious angels
Going to heaven!
"Going to him! Happy letter! Tell him –
Good night! which put the candle out?
Great streets of silence led away
Have you got a brook in your little heart,
He ate and drank the precious words,
He fumbles at your spirit
He preached upon "breadth" till it argued him narrow, –
He put the belt around my life, –
He touched me, so I live to know
Heart not so heavy as mine,
Heart, we will forget him!
Heaven is what I cannot reach!
Her final summer was it,
High from the earth I heard a bird;
His bill an auger is,
Hope is a subtle glutton;
Hope is the thing with feathers
How dare the robins sing,
How happy is the little stone
How many times these low feet staggered,
How still the bells in steeples stand,
How the old mountains drip with sunset,
I asked no other thing,
I breathed enough to learn the trick,
I bring an unaccustomed wine
I can wade grief,
I cannot live with you,
I died for beauty, but was scarce
I dreaded that first robin so,
I envy seas whereon he rides,
I felt a clearing in my mind
I felt a funeral in my brain,
I found the phrase to every thought
I gained it so,
I gave myself to him,
I had a daily bliss
I had a guinea golden;
I had been hungry all the years;
I had no cause to be awake,
I had no time to hate, because
I have a king who does not speak;
I have no life but this,
I have not told my garden yet,
I heard a fly buzz when I died;
I held a jewel in my fingers
I hide myself within my flower,
I know a place where summer strives
I know some lonely houses off the road
I know that he exists
I like a look of agony,
I like to see it lap the miles,
I live with him, I see his face;
I lived on dread; to those who know
I lost a world the other day.
I many times thought peace had come,
I meant to find her when I came;
I meant to have but modest needs,
I measure every grief I meet
I never hear the word "escape"
I never lost as much but twice,
I never saw a moor,
I noticed people disappeared,
I read my sentence steadily,
I reason, earth is short,
I shall know why, when time is over,
I should have been too glad, I see,
I should not dare to leave my friend,
I sing to use the waiting,
I started early, took my dog,
I stepped from plank to plank
I taste a liquor never brewed,
I think just how my shape will rise
I think the hemlock likes to stand
I took my power in my hand.
I went to heaven, –
I went to thank her,
I wish I knew that woman's name,
I wonder if the sepulchre
I worked for chaff, and earning wheat
I years had been from home,
I'll tell you how the sun rose, –
I'm ceded, I've stopped being theirs;
I'm nobody! Who are you?
I'm wife; I've finished that,
I've got an arrow here;
I've seen a dying eye
If I can stop one heart from breaking,
If I may have it when it's dead
If I should die,
If I shouldn't be alive
If anybody's friend be dead,
If recollecting were forgetting,
If the foolish call them 'flowers,'
If tolling bell I ask the cause.
If you were coming in the fall,
Immortal is an ample word
In lands I never saw, they say,
Is Heaven a physician?
Is bliss, then, such abyss
It can't be summer, – that got through;
It dropped so low in my regard
It is an honorable thought,
It makes no difference abroad,
It might be easier
It sifts from leaden sieves,
It sounded as if the streets were running,
It struck me every day
It tossed and tossed, –
It was not death, for I stood up,
It was too late for man,
It's like the light, –
It's such a little thing to weep,
Just lost when I was saved!
Lay this laurel on the one
Let down the bars, O Death!
Let me not mar that perfect dream
Life, and Death, and Giants
Like mighty footlights burned the red
Like trains of cars on tracks of plush
Look back on time with kindly eyes,
Love is anterior to life,
Me! Come! My dazzled face
Mine by the right of the white election!
Mine enemy is growing old, –
Morning is the place for dew,
Morns like these we parted;
Much madness is divinest sense
Musicians wrestle everywhere:
My cocoon tightens, colors tease,
My country need not change her gown,
My friend must be a bird,
My life closed twice before its close;
My river runs to thee:
My worthiness is all my doubt,
Nature rarer uses yellow
Nature, the gentlest mother,
New feet within my garden go,
No brigadier throughout the year
No rack can torture me,
Not any higher stands the grave
Not in this world to see his face
Not knowing when the dawn will come
Not with a club the heart is broken,
Of all the souls that stand create
Of all the sounds despatched abroad,
Of bronze and blaze
Of tribulation these are they
On such a night, or such a night,
On the bleakness of my lot
On this long storm the rainbow rose,
On this wondrous sea,
One blessing had I, than the rest
One day is there of the series
One dignity delays for all,
One need not be a chamber to be haunted,
One of the ones that Midas touched,
Our journey had advanced;
Our lives are Swiss, –
Our share of night to bear,
Pain has an element of blank;
Perhaps you'd like to buy a flower?
Pigmy seraphs gone astray,
Pink, small, and punctual,
Pompless no life can pass away;
Poor little heart!
Portraits are to daily faces
Prayer is the little implement
Presentiment is that long shadow on the lawn
Proud of my broken heart since thou didst break it,
Read, sweet, how others strove,
Remembrance has a rear and front, –
Remorse is memory awake,
Safe in their alabaster chambers,
She died, – this was the way she died;
She laid her docile crescent down,
She rose to his requirement, dropped
She slept beneath a tree
She sweeps with many–colored brooms,
She went as quiet as the dew
Sleep is supposed to be,
So bashful when I spied her,
So proud she was to die
Softened by Time's consummate plush,
Some keep the Sabbath going to church;
Some rainbow coming from the fair!
Some things that fly there be, –
Some, too fragile for winter winds,
Soul, wilt thou toss again?
South winds jostle them,
Split the lark and you'll find the music,
Step lightly on this narrow spot!
Success is counted sweetest
Summer for thee grant I may be
Superfluous were the sun
Superiority to fate
Surgeons must be very careful
Sweet hours have perished here;
Sweet is the swamp with its secrets,
Taken from men this morning,
Talk with prudence to a beggar
That I did always love,
That is solemn we have ended, –
That short, potential stir
That such have died enables us
The bat is dun with wrinkled wings
The bee is not afraid of me,
The body grows outside, –
The bone that has no marrow;
The brain is wider than the sky,
The brain within its groove
The bustle in a house
The butterfly's assumption–gown,
The clouds their backs together laid,
The cricket sang,
The daisy follows soft the sun,
The day came slow, till five o'clock,
The distance that the dead have gone
The dying need but little, dear, –
The farthest thunder that I heard
The gentian weaves her fringes,
The grass so little has to do, –
The grave my little cottage is,
The heart asks pleasure first,
The last night that she lived,
The leaves, like women, interchange
The moon is distant from the sea,
The moon was but a chin of gold
The morns are meeker than they were,
The mountain sat upon the plain
The murmur of a bee
The murmuring of bees has ceased;
The mushroom is the elf of plants,
The nearest dream recedes, unrealized.
The night was wide, and furnished scant
The one that could repeat the summer day
The only ghost I ever saw
The past is such a curious creature,
The pedigree of honey
The rat is the concisest tenant.
The reticent volcano keeps
The robin is the one
The rose did caper on her cheek,
The show is not the show,
The skies can't keep their secret!
The sky is low, the clouds are mean,
The soul selects her own society,
The soul should always stand ajar,
The soul unto itself
The spider as an artist
The springtime's pallid landscape
The stimulus, beyond the grave
The sun just touched the morning;
The sun kept setting, setting still;
The thought beneath so slight a film
The way I read a letter 's this:
The wind begun to rock the grass
Their height in heaven comforts not,
There came a day at summer's full
There came a wind like a bugle;
There is a flower that bees prefer,
There is a shame of nobleness
There is a word
There is no frigate like a book
There's a certain slant of light,
There's been a death in the opposite house
There's something quieter than sleep
These are the days when birds come back,
They dropped like flakes, they dropped like stars,
They say that 'time assuages,' –
They won't frown always, – some sweet day
This is my letter to the world,
This is the land the sunset washes,
This merit hath the worst, –
This was in the white of the year,
This world is not conclusion;
Though I get home how late, how late!
Three weeks passed since I had seen her, –
Through the straight pass of suffering
'T is so much joy! 'T is so much joy!
'T is sunrise, little maid, hast thou
'T is whiter than an Indian pipe,
Tie the strings to my life, my Lord,
To fight aloud is very brave,
To hang our head ostensibly,
To hear an oriole sing
To help our bleaker parts
To know just how he suffered would be dear;
To learn the transport by the pain,
To lose one's faith surpasses
To lose thee, sweeter than to gain
To make a prairie it takes a clover and one bee, –
To my quick ear the leaves conferred;
To venerate the simple days
Triumph may be of several kinds.
'T is little I could care for pearls
'T was a long parting, but the time
'T was just this time last year I died.
'T was later when the summer went
'T was such a little, little boat
Two butterflies went out at noon
Two swimmers wrestled on the spar
Undue significance a starving man attaches
Unto my books so good to turn
Upon the gallows hung a wretch,
Victory comes late,
Wait till the majesty of Death
Water is taught by thirst;
We cover thee, sweet face.
We learn in the retreating
We like March, his shoes are purple,
We never know how high we are
We never know we go, – when we are going
We outgrow love like other things
We play at paste,
We thirst at first, – 't is Nature's act;
Went up a year this evening!
What if I say I shall not wait?
What inn is this
What mystery pervades a well!
What soft, cherubic creatures
When I hoped I feared,
When I was small, a woman died.
When night is almost done,
When roses cease to bloom, dear,
Where every bird is bold to go,
Where ships of purple gently toss
Whether my bark went down at sea,
While I was fearing it, it came,
Who has not found the heaven below
Who never lost, are unprepared
Who never wanted, – maddest joy
Who robbed the woods,
"Whose are the little beds," I asked,
Wild nights! Wild nights!
Will there really be a morning?
Within my reach!
You cannot put a fire out;
You left me, sweet, two legacies, –
You've seen balloons set, haven't you?
Your riches taught me poverty.
